summary: Every valid result contains exactly N explicit output characters, giving an Omega(N) construction lower bound, and the accepted-form method performs one search followed by one linear result construction.

optimality.argument: The function must return a string of length N, so materializing the explicit output requires writing N character positions. Searching for the boundary and constructing the reversed prefix plus unchanged suffix each take only linear work.

replacement_checks.evidence: The required return value has exactly N characters, establishing an Omega(N) output-construction lower bound in the explicit-string model.
The reference performs one linear first-occurrence search and constructs exactly one reversed prefix, one suffix, and the N-character result.
Authored cases cover the first character, repeated occurrences, an absent character, a one-character word, a whole-word reversal, an internal boundary, and maximum legal length.
